sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try clicking the top red marker and see how the non-marker rows change
# First, save current state
def get_non_marker_colors(frame):
    def get_row_colors(row):
        ascii_lines = frame.ascii.split('\n')
        line = ascii_lines[row]
        colors = []
        i = 0
        while i < len(line):
            ch = line[i]
            if ch not in ('c', 'G', 'N', 'B', 'R'):
                j = i
                while j < len(line) and line[j] == ch:
                    j += 1
                colors.append(ch)
                i = j
            else:
                i += 1
        return tuple(colors)
    return {row: get_row_colors(row) for row in [20, 23, 29, 32, 38, 41, 44]}

before = get_non_marker_colors(current_frame)
print("Before clicking top red:")
for row, c in before.items():
    print(f"  Row {row}: {c}")

# Click top red marker
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 20}])

after = get_non_marker_colors(current_frame)
print("\nAfter clicking top red:")
for row, c in after.items():
    changed = "CHANGED" if c != before[row] else ""
    print(f"  Row {row}: {c} {changed}")

# Check which rows changed
print("\nChanged rows:")
for row in before:
    if before[row] != after[row]:
        print(f"  Row {row}: {before[row]} -> {after[row]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

# Let me try clicking the top red marker and see how the non-marker rows change
# First, save current state
def get_non_marker_colors(frame):
    def get_row_colors(row):
        ascii_lines = frame.ascii.split('\n')
        line = ascii_lines[row]
        colors = []
        i = 0
        while i < len(line):
            ch = line[i]
            if ch not in ('c', 'G', 'N', 'B', 'R'):
                j = i
                while j < len(line) and line[j] == ch:
                    j += 1
                colors.append(ch)
                i = j
            else:
                i += 1
        return tuple(colors)
    return {row: get_row_colors(row) for row in [20, 23, 29, 32, 38, 41, 44]}

before = get_non_marker_colors(current_frame)
